title: Gate Maintenance, Upgrades, Metrics, and Capstone · Gate Defense
summary: Operate a reusable repository template without letting gates rot or overwhelm contributors. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reusable quality-gated repository template. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure defect escape, false positives, duration, flakiness, suppression age, and developer friction.
Schedule compatibility-tested tool and runtime upgrades with rollback paths.
Raise standards gradually with changed-code gates and expiring debt records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Quality Metrics evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Quality Metrics assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Measure defect escape, false positives, duration, flakiness, suppression age, and developer friction. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates maintenance. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Quality Metrics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a reusable quality-gated repository template.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: More failed checks indicate a healthier repository. This fails the Quality Metrics contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Biome Linter Rules (Biome 2.5) — Controls modern JavaScript and TypeScript formatting, correctness, accessibility, and unused-code lint examples.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

stimulus.title: Capstone Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Capstone Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend each gate by risk, ownership, feedback time, evidence, and maintenance plan.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Quality Metrics to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Quality Metrics.
